entry.title: Where Do The Dead People Go?

link.title: Where Do The Dead People Go?

entry.content:
I wonder where the dead people go? Do they carry any luggages with them? And what is their mode of transport?

Growing up, my idea of death has undergone several transformations. I once read somewhere about death, and how, as we know it, it is not the opposite of life but rather a part of it, the ultimate conclusion. Since then I have never seen death in the same light I used to see it in before.

At a certain point in my life, I was so fascinated with the idea of death that I read almost every single philosopher’s work on it and I thought I knew things.

When I was a child, I used to watch action movies and when I saw bullets being fired in them, I used to imagine myself in that situation and I would think, ‘Oh how much of an idiot they’re, why don’t they just move away and dodge the bullet?’. Totally unaware of a bullet’s velocity, the child me used to live happily in his bullet-resistive imagination. But one day experiencing the death of a person so close caught me by my collar and threw me out of that bullet-resistive imagination of mine.

Imagine this – you grow up watching someone be a part of your life, you laugh with them, go on walks together, watch movies on weekends and eat rasmalais. You see them smiling, crying, singing, dancing and experiencing every single possible human emotion and one day you wake up and find them lying on a 7 feet bed completely frozen in their position with so much calmness on their face that it starts messing up with your turbulent little head. You don’t realize what’s wrong, why the air around you is vibrating with the sobbing of familiar faces, but then the realization hits you, you feel it in your bones! You try to scream but your scream attenuates internally scratching your throat. And that’s when you come to know what death is!

I feel like people are born colorless, they get coloured over time with memories and experiences, good and bad. And each of those colors is reminiscent of people you made the memory with.

I guess I will never know where the dead people go and if they’re even allowed to carry their colors with them from the land of living but what I do know is the fact that their beautiful and vivid colors will never faint off me as long as I am alive and breathing.

On experiencing death and loss
entry.summary: I wonder where the dead people go? Do they carry any luggages with them? And what is their mode of transport?

entry.title: A Letter to The Future Self

link.title: A Letter to The Future Self

entry.content:
Dear Jigyasu,

I hope you, the future me, are thriving in a life you envisioned for yourself. Don’t worry, I won’t be disappointed even if you have messed up somewhere in between, though I wanna know if you’re still satisfied with yourself or not?

Writing down this letter actually made me reflect on a lot of things in the present. I find myself navigating through a vast ocean of experiences and sometimes it feels like there is no pole star to guide me through. I guess this is how adulthood feels like, you’re experiencing things that for you are very unique in their nature and somehow you are expected to get most of them right. You experience failures, success, love, loss, betrayal and all sorts of possible human emotions, and I suppose these experiences are not bad afterall, these are what makes you human and alive, no?

More often than I would actually want it to happen, I find myself engulfed in a cloud of sadness and melancholy and you know what’s cool about these clouds? The fact that they’re clouds! They always float away, it’s their very nature. I have started to identify these states of sadness and happiness as a group of clouds, they always float away and replace themselves. Sometimes it rains when it gets too heavy and at other times it’s so warm and shiny that you could even feel the butterflies and birds rejoicing with you.

Jigyasu, I want you to know something, I don’t know if you still are exactly the same in the way I feel things but I want you to be very grateful about people, things and experiences in life. You’d know of the lovely girl that I have because I like to believe that even years later we are still together, growing and thriving in love. I can only hope so but hope is a pretty pretty thing.

Someday I picture you sitting in a cafe somewhere in Vienna or Amsterdam watching the world go by as you read this letter addressed to you by me, the past you and while you do that, I want you to be very proud of yourself, you made it, bud! However, if you never get to experience this, I still want you to be equally proud, you tried your best.

I love you for not being an alpha, sigma or chad and just being the guy you’re. Feel things, be vulnerable. Preserve your true self, the younger you finds it beautiful.

I wanna bid adieu here but before I end this, I wanna highlight the opening line of Billy Joel’s Vienna, “Slow down you crazy child”. Slow down somewhere down the line and be gentle with yourself.

With optimism and love, Your Current Self

January 13th, 2024

entry.title: Hello, world
entry.content: Hello! This is a post to test out the blog :)
entry.summary: Hello! This is a post to test out the blog :)
